styleHead: STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation
castCountLine: CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Old_Man. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.
actionText: INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. THE OLD MAN's breathing-thin, listening. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator says "He had been saying to himself - It is nothing but the wind in the chimney -". Character_The_Old_Man <IMAGE_1> still wears loose white cotton nightshirt, barefoot Camera directive: Close-up creeping in, 85mm lens, old man's rigid listening face silhouette trying to dismiss the fear. Performance: Acting intensity 6/10: Self-soothing furrowed brow, uncertain eye-widen, mouth working Optics: f/2.0 shallow depth of field, soft falloff into darkness Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, pitch-black voids, cool-teal shadows, faint warm amber lantern spill
prompt:
STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation

CHARACTER VARIABLES (use these identities consistently; do not redesign faces or wardrobe):
- Character_The_Narrator [The Narrator]: Also present (not shot focus); keep identity consistent: Lean man of middle years (about 40–50), pale sallow skin,....
- Character_The_Old_Man <IMAGE_1> [The Old Man]: Frail elderly man, thin stooped build, sparse white hair, deeply lined pale face; one pale blue eye with a dull filmy veil, the other eye ordinary; wears a plain white period nightshirt. Visual lock: Always elderly, white-haired, frail; signature constant is the single pale blue eye with dull filmy veil that must not drift to clear blue or to the Narrator's face; nightshirt is his default on-screen garment. Voice: Elderly male; thin, reedy, low energy; short startled phrases; weak breath support; same fragile voice in the single spoken line and any murmur. Match appearance of reference <IMAGE_1> exactly.

CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Old_Man. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.

AUDIO: REQUIRED native Grok off-camera voiceover. Character_The_Narrator narrates exactly: "He had been saying to himself - It is nothing but the wind in the chimney -". Start speaking immediately with "He" — do not skip, delay, or swallow the opening word. After the last word, hold a brief natural pause with a closed mouth (about half a second); do not freeze mid-syllable or trail into empty staring. Do not lip-sync on-screen cast to this VO. Ambient bed: thin breathing. VOICE LOCK Character_The_Narrator: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

CONTEXT (prior clip in scene — new cast plate refs attached; match location/lighting if still valid; identity from CHARACTER VARIABLES + locked plates only):
INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. THE OLD MAN's breathing-thin, listening. Character_The_Old_Man still wears loose white cotton nightshirt, barefoot Camera directive: Extreme close-up on old man's chest silhouette, 100mm macro, thin listening breaths rising and falling. Performance: Acting intensity 7/10: Thin shallow terrified breathing, lips parted dry, eyes darting Optics: f/1.4 shallow depth of field, creamy soft bokeh Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, pitch-black voids, cool-teal shadows, faint warm amber lantern spill
